highlights.item: Phase 07 is now explicit so the first-party CRM rebuild is tracked as its own numbered delivery stream instead of being hidden across phases 05 and 06.
A capability map now translates HubSpot primitives into first-party Payload collections, Next.js internal APIs, Stripe webhook reconciliation, and Payload-jobs-driven Resend sequences.

The CRM foundation schema now exists in repo with `accounts`, `contacts`, `leads`, `opportunities`, `crm-activities`, `crm-tasks`, `crm-sequences`, and `sequence-enrollments`, including commercial account fields and an in-app sequence-definition model.
Public form submissions and quote lifecycle hooks now create or update first-party CRM records through small internal service files instead of leaving the sync layer as a no-op placeholder.

`07-02` now has a real server-side workspace layer: `src/lib/crm/workspace/*` plus admin-only routes at `src/app/api/internal/crm/workspace/route.ts` and `src/app/api/internal/crm/record/route.ts` for queue summaries and list/detail reads.

`07-03` now has a compact CRM tab inside `/ops`, built from smaller command-center components and a dedicated `src/components/portal/crm/CrmWorkspace.tsx` surface that keeps details in the left rail instead of opening overlays.
Focused validation also started under `07-06`: CRM workspace integration tests plus Playwright admin/frontend coverage now pass alongside typecheck and production build.
The CRM workspace now supports server-backed search plus inline quick actions for task status updates and opportunity stage advancement via small admin-only mutation routes.

The left detail rail is now structured for CRM records instead of flattening everything into plain text, with compact tabs, richer company fields, and inline internal note logging backed by `crm-activities`.

`07-04` billing planning is now concrete: Stripe-hosted invoices and portal-first self-service are the default customer-facing payment UX for account holders, while Payload remains the operational billing ledger and must support commercial net-30 monthly invoicing plus onsite/out-of-band payments, discounts, credits, and refunds.

Company-account customer scope is now explicit instead of inferred: `users` carry an `account` relationship, quotes/invoices/service plans/appointments carry the same account reference, those records auto-resolve their account when possible, and an admin preview toolbar can impersonate customer users across the frontend and portal without exposing `/ops` or admin docs to the effective non-admin user.
Phase planning now includes an explicit operating-rhythm workflow with multi-role employee duties, queue/SLA expectations, and the required next-action contract so daily ownership is not implicit.
Reminder strategy is now explicit: fastest rollout uses Payload jobs + Resend to each worker's preferred existing inbox (personal or company), while Gmail/Microsoft OAuth remains optional later work.

Boundary policy is now reinforced in planning as a first-class requirement: cross-user/company views and internal docs remain staff-only; customers remain self/account-scoped, with company-admin customers limited to their own company user management.

The operating-rhythm workflow now includes tighter v1 SLA proposals (lead/quote/schedule/invoice), explicit support/refund SLA classes, and a task-first role model suited to a two-person startup team.
Discount policy planning is now explicit: support both account-wide defaults and user-level overrides, with precedence and audit behavior tracked as a dedicated open billing decision/task.

Ops IA planning now requires a route split for dense modules and a conventional shadcn app shell: command center becomes current-user duties, while CRM/scorecard/milestones/assets/today board move to dedicated `/ops/*` pages.
Portal shell hardening is now tracked to prevent sidebar/content overlap, horizontal overflow, and hidden impersonation toolbar states.

A new engineering-conventions track now exists for quality guardrails: icon-forward form/action affordances, reduced copy-heavy faded UI patterns, centralized literals via typed shared constants, and extraction triggers for large files/reusable utilities.
Conventions are now measurable and pattern-backed: extraction thresholds (repeat counts and size guardrails) plus a shared catalog of preferred app patterns for ops/customer implementations.
A refactor extraction roadmap now anchors the target folder structure and prioritized split queue so architecture cleanup can happen incrementally alongside feature delivery.

next.item: Keep `07-01` moving by tightening dedupe, promotion, and owner-routing rules across form intake, quotes, and commercial accounts, then backfill any existing production customer records that still lack the new `account` relationship.
Deepen `07-02` with richer write actions beyond note logging, especially owner reassignment, lead qualification, and broader search/filter coverage.
Deepen `07-03` with stronger commercial/company detail, richer queue states, and inline operator actions without regressing the compact non-overlapping layout.

Execute `07-04` in slices: Stripe customer and invoice mapping first, then out-of-band payment recording and billing adjustments, then commercial monthly consolidated billing, then portal-session links from the customer account.
Follow with `07-05`: Payload-jobs/Resend sequences after billing rails are stable.
Use `07-06` to seed realistic CRM scenarios and broaden coverage into billing, scheduling, and customer account flows.
Deliver `07-07` operating-rhythm outputs: role matrix, SLA table, queue priorities, and escalation ownership rules mapped to first-party task records.
Deliver `07-08` boundary hardening: audit customer/admin route guards, account scope enforcement, and company-admin membership limits.
Run `07-09` MCP operational checks in Codex/Cursor contexts so planning and implementation sessions can rely on payload_local and image_gen_local without setup drift.
Execute `07-10` discount policy design: finalize account-vs-user precedence, approval controls, and support/refund interaction before implementation.
Execute `07-11` shell hardening so sidebar/inset/toolbars behave like a conventional app layout with no overlap regressions.
Execute `07-12` ops route split and convert command center into a "my duties" hub with deep links/query params to specialized views.
Execute `07-14` conventions adoption incrementally on touched surfaces so large files shrink and repeated literal patterns are consolidated over normal feature work.
Use the measurable thresholds and pattern catalog in implementation reviews so DRY/SOLID checks are explicit instead of subjective.
Execute `07-15` roadmap slices in priority order to migrate ops/auth/crm/dashboard modules toward the target folder structure without stop-the-world rewrites.
